expectations.text: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c uses Fortran-style flat params: void cbksub_(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *istate, int *nflag, int *errflg)

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
expectations.evidence: No event-based switch; uses CORRECTOR_PASS callback event concept without ev_* enum constants.

expectations.text: CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
expectations.evidence: No event handling; no ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 handling.

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
expectations.evidence: Uses Fortran-style flat params: void vfosub_(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *dflag, int *iflag, double *result)

expectations.text: VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: No iflag guard present; function body directly reads cached value.

expectations.text: VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
expectations.evidence: No dflag handling; always reads from cached global without any fallback.

expectations.text: Both files use c_errmes for error handling
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c uses c_errmes but vfosub.c does not; assertion requires both files.

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: Uses raw cl /c and link /DLL on Windows; raw gcc -shared on Linux. mdi.bat is called but only as env setup, not as the build tool.

expectations.text: Build instructions mention setting up the Adams environment before running mdi.bat
expectations.evidence: Does not use mdi.bat as build tool; calls 'call mdi.bat' for env setup only then uses raw compiler commands.
